SchoolBus.title: SchoolBus
SchoolBus.description: Represents a school bus with its details.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SchoolBus entity.

busNumber.description: The number assigned to the bus for identification.

capacity.description: The seating capacity of the bus (e.g., 45, 25, 15).

type.description: The type or model of the bus.

Student.title: Student
Student.description: Represents a student and their associated information.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Student entity.

arrivalBusId.description: Reference to SchoolBus for arrival. (Relationship: SchoolBus 1:N Student (Arrival))

departureBusId.description: Reference to SchoolBus for departure. (Relationship: SchoolBus 1:N Student (Departure))

monday.description: Indicates whether the student rides the bus on Monday.

tuesday.description: Indicates whether the student rides the bus on Tuesday.

wednesday.description: Indicates whether the student rides the bus on Wednesday.

thursday.description: Indicates whether the student rides the bus on Thursday.

friday.description: Indicates whether the student rides the bus on Friday.

Destination.title: Destination
Destination.description: Represents a destination or stop along a bus route.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Destination entity.

address.description: The physical address of the destination.

BusRoute.title: BusRoute
BusRoute.description: Represents a specific route for a school bus, including the order of destinations.

id.description: Unique identifier for the BusRoute entity.

destinationIds.description: References to Destinations in order. (Relationship: Destination 1:N BusRoute)

routeName.description: The name of the bus route.

type.description: The type of the bus route (e.g., arrival, departure).

Absence.title: Absence
Absence.description: Represents a record of a student's absence on a particular day.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Absence entity.

GroupLeader.title: GroupLeader
GroupLeader.description: Represents the designation of a student as a group leader, including volunteer time tracking.

id.description: Unique identifier for the GroupLeader entity.

startDate.description: The date the student was designated as group leader.

totalVolunteerMinutes.description: The total volunteer time in minutes accumulated by the student as a group leader.

definition.description: Stores school bus details. Allows admin to create and manage buses.

params.name: schoolBusId
params.description: Unique identifier for the school bus.

definition.description: Stores destination information, managed by admins.
Stores bus route information, managed by admins.

params.name: busRouteId
params.description: Unique identifier for the bus route.

definition.description: Stores individual student data. Includes references to destination and arrival/departure buses.
Tracks student absences. Accessible to teachers/admins.

params.name: absenceId
params.description: Unique identifier for the absence record.

definition.description: Tracks group leader assignments and volunteer time.

params.name: groupLeaderInfoId
params.description: Unique identifier for the group leader assignment.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to manage school bus operations effectively, addressing admin, teacher, and student needs.  Authorization Independence is achieved through path-based ownership for user-specific data (students).  The structure also leverages denormalization where necessary to avoid complex `get()` calls in security rules.

*   `/schoolBuses`: Stores school bus details. Allows admin to create and manage buses. QAPs are met as only authorized admins can `list` and `create` here.
*   `/destinations`: Stores destination information, also managed by admins.
*   `/busRoutes`: Stores bus route information, managed by admins.
*   `/students/{studentId}`:  Stores individual student data. This supports the ability to assign students to destinations and arrival/departure buses. Path-based ownership ensures only the user themselves can access/modify their data, meeting QAPs.
*   `/students/{studentId}/absences/{absenceId}`: Tracks student absences. Accessible to teachers/admins who need to mark absences. The nested structure allows for efficient querying of absences for a specific student. Path-based ownership ensures only authorized teachers/admins can access student absence records.
*   `/students/{studentId}/groupLeaderInfo/{groupLeaderInfoId}`: Tracks group leader assignments and volunteer time.  Accessible to teachers/admins. Nested under `/students/{studentId}` to simplify security rules.

This structure avoids hierarchical authorization by directly associating student data with the appropriate bus route and destination through denormalized fields. This avoids the need for complex `get()` calls in Firestore security rules, improving performance and security.
